title: Agent runtime test note: durable checkpoints move earlier in architecture
summary: TEST MATERIAL. Teams comparing agent frameworks are evaluating checkpoint boundaries, idempotent retries, and operator recovery before tool breadth.

title: Agent toolkit test note: local prototype assumes full Node.js APIs
summary: TEST MATERIAL. The candidate toolkit uses child_process, a persistent background process, and writes checkpoints to a local filesystem. Some orchestration logic is portable, but its runtime adapter is not edge-safe without migration.

title: Evaluation test note: task traces replace answer-only scores
summary: TEST MATERIAL. An internal evaluation sample records context trimming, tool failures, repair attempts, cost, and user corrections for each run.

title: Structured output test note: schema support still needs application validation
summary: TEST MATERIAL. A model provider added JSON-schema output but documents that complex generations can still fail validation.

title: Small editor model test note: constrained prompts reduce repair rate
summary: TEST MATERIAL. A benchmark reports that fewer optional fields and explicit source identifiers improve valid JSON rates for an 8B editor model.

title: ZX Signal test note: confirmed memory is separated from proposals
summary: TEST MATERIAL. The proposed design stores candidate, entry, and event records separately so no comment becomes active context without user confirmation.

title: ZX Signal test note: D1 batch can switch active briefing versions atomically
summary: TEST MATERIAL. The persistence proposal uses prepared statements and a transaction batch to supersede the active daily briefing and insert its replacement.

title: Deployment-fit test note: runtime constraints become an experiment gate
summary: TEST MATERIAL. A zxlab checklist asks about Web API support, Node.js compatibility, persistent processes, filesystem assumptions, storage migration, and cost before adoption.

title: API boundary test note: public reads and protected writes
summary: TEST MATERIAL. The Signal API proposal leaves daily briefings readable while requiring a development token or Cloudflare Access policy for generation, comments, and memory changes.

title: Observability test note: metadata without personal text
summary: TEST MATERIAL. Model invocation logs keep task, model, prompt version, duration, status, and usage while excluding full comments, memories, and model responses.

title: Memory market test note: supply discipline matters more than one quote
summary: TEST MATERIAL. A sample market brief compares inventory, capital expenditure, utilization, and HBM capacity migration instead of treating a daily spot quote as a cycle signal.

title: Commodity test note: gold and copper express different risks
summary: TEST MATERIAL. Gold sensitivity is framed around monetary and geopolitical risk while copper depends more on industrial demand, inventories, and new supply.

title: Inventory test note: channel restocking is not end demand
summary: TEST MATERIAL. A distributor survey shows a short restocking pulse, while end-market orders remain unchanged in the same sample period.

title: Capital expenditure test note: announced capacity arrives with a lag
summary: TEST MATERIAL. A sample scenario separates announced fab spending from equipment installation, qualification, and actual output dates.

title: Low-novelty test note: unchanged weekly consensus
summary: TEST MATERIAL. This candidate intentionally repeats the prior week's broad market consensus and should be omitted when no new evidence is present.
